text: HIV infected women should be counseled about the risk of HIV transmission through breast milk and the risks and benefits of each feeding method, with specific guidance in selecting the option most likely to be suitable for their situation. In any case, mixed feeding i.e. breast-feeding along with other feeds should be strictly discouraged as it increases the risk of HIV transmission. /c63Start breast-feeding within an hour after delivery and do not discard colostrum. /c63Breast-feed exclusively (not even water) for a minimum of six months if the growth of the infant is adequate. /c63Continue breast-feeding in addition to nutrient-rich complementary foods (weaning foods), preferably up to 2 years. /c63Breast-feed the infant frequently and on demand to establish and maintain good milk
